note: An illustrative hand-run of the Claude Code loop as raw curl calls — one user prompt, two /v1/messages round-trips. Request bodies are sketched; the shapes (cache_control placement, tool_use/tool_result, stop_reason, usage cache fields) are real. Source lines valid at the pinned revision in docs/methodology.md.

lines.text: # Turn 1 — assemble the first request and fire it

lines.annotation: Two cache breakpoints: last tool block + last user block. System prefix cached globally.

lines.text: event: content_block_delta   text: "I'll update the function."
event: content_block_start   { type: tool_use, id: toolu_01A.., name: "Edit" }

lines.annotation: but Claude Code detects tool_use from the stream, not this field

lines.text: stop_reason = tool_use → the model wants a tool. Gate it (canUseTool), then run it locally.
# Turn 2 — append the assistant turn + tool_result, fire again
Identical system + tools + CLAUDE.md prefix as turn 1 → cache HIT. messages now end with the assistant's tool_use and the matching tool_result.

lines.annotation: the prefix was read from cache, not re-billed

lines.text: event: content_block_delta   text: "Done — the function now handles the empty case."
end_turn, no tool_use → the loop exits. One user prompt, two API round-trips, one cached prefix.
